findings.title: Sensitive environment variables exposed
findings.recommendation: Remove secrets from environment; use a secrets manager.

findings.title: No high-risk ports detected on localhost
findings.description: None of the commonly-abused ports are listening.

findings.title: File integrity baseline captured (1 files)
findings.description: SHA-256 hashes recorded for monitored files.
findings.recommendation: Store this baseline securely and re-run to detect tampering.

findings.title: No source files found to scan
Configure firewall
findings.recommendation: Use ufw (Ubuntu) or firewalld (RHEL): deny all inbound by default, allow only required ports.

findings.title: Use SSH key authentication
findings.recommendation: Set `PasswordAuthentication no` in sshd_config after placing your public key.

findings.title: Enable audit logging
findings.recommendation: Install and start auditd; configure rules for privilege escalation and file access.

findings.title: Restrict SUID/SGID binaries
findings.recommendation: Run `find / -perm /6000 -type f` and remove SUID from binaries that don't need it.

findings.title: Enable SELinux or AppArmor
findings.recommendation: Mandatory access control limits damage from compromised processes.

findings.title: Disable unused services
findings.recommendation: Run `systemctl list-units --type=service` and disable anything not required.

findings.title: Use Fail2Ban
findings.recommendation: Install Fail2Ban to auto-block IPs with repeated failed authentication attempts.

findings.title: Keep kernel up-to-date
findings.recommendation: Kernel exploits are a common privilege escalation path; patch regularly.
